tests.name: init_sh_non_interactive_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh --non-interactive completed successfully for personal/web/standard with --git-host other and fake remote.

tests.name: context7_detection
tests.details: verify-install.sh from project dir reports Context7 MCP configured; 52/52 checks pass.

tests.name: framework_self_guard_active
tests.details: init.sh from inside $FRAMEWORK exits 1 with 'Refusing to operate inside the Solo Orchestrator framework repo' message (PR #18 verified).

tests.name: baseline_phase1_steps_completed
tests.details: All 5 phase1_architecture steps completed (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized).

tests.name: baseline_phase_advance_to_2
tests.details: Phase advanced to 2 manually because no auto-advance script exists; check-phase-gate.sh reports consistent. U-D from prior sweep remains open (already known).

tests.name: baseline_feature_recorded
tests.details: uat-feat-1 build loop completed all 6 steps; commit succeeded; test-gate.sh --record-feature added it to .features_completed array.

tests.name: upgrade_command_exits_zero
tests.details: intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment organizational (run as project-local script) exited with code 1. The upgrade transformations all completed successfully (phase-state, tool-preferences, CLAUDE.md, PROJECT_INTAKE.md, APPROVAL_LOG.md were updated and committed; verify-install passed). The non-zero exit comes from the final 'Refreshing framework helper scripts' block at upgrade-project.sh:1469-1480, where SCRIPT_DIR resolves to the project's own scripts/ directory when invoked locally, so 'cp scripts/pending-approval.sh scripts/pending-approval.sh' is a self-copy that exits 1 ('are identical (not copied)') and aborts the script under set -euo pipefail before 'Upgrade complete.' is printed and before scripts/validate.sh post-upgrade pass runs. U-E (poc_mode lookup) is NOT regressed — phase-state read worked. This is a NEW Critical bug introduced by the BL-009/BL-015 helper-refresh block in PR #17.

tests.name: new_required_artifacts_listed
tests.details: Upgrade output prints 'Next steps for organizational deployment: 1. Fill in the Pre-Phase 0 organizational pre-conditions in APPROVAL_LOG.md / 2. Complete Section 8 of PROJECT_INTAKE.md (governance pre-flight) / 3. Assign approval authorities for each phase gate'.

tests.name: pending_approval_sentinel_respected
tests.details: With .claude/pending-approval.json in place, intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment organizational exits with [FAIL] upgrade blocked — pending user decision. PR #17 U-F fix verified. After scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve, upgrade proceeds.

tests.name: upgrade_changelog_present_in_script
tests.details: scripts/upgrade-project.sh header includes Changelog entries for BL-006 (2026-04-24) and BL-015 (2026-04-25).

tests.name: no_template_placeholders_in_output
tests.details: grep of upgrade log for __PROJECT_NAME__, __PLATFORM__, __LANGUAGE__, __TRACK__ returned 0 hits; no U-I regression in this config.

tests.name: deployment_field_written_to_phase_state
tests.details: After personal -> organizational upgrade, .deployment in .claude/phase-state.json remains 'personal' (NOT 'organizational'). The phase-state update block at upgrade-project.sh:535-559 writes data['track'] and data['last_upgrade'] but never writes data['deployment'] — TARGET_DEPLOYMENT is not even passed as an argv to that python heredoc. Confirms U-J from prior sweep is still open.

tests.name: poc_mode_set_to_target
tests.details: Target was private_poc, but .poc_mode in phase-state.json remains null after upgrade. The intake-wizard --upgrade-deployment flag only accepts personal|organizational and has no way to specify private_poc vs sponsored_poc target — it forwards to upgrade-project.sh --deployment organizational, which sets deployment to organizational but does not set poc_mode for new POC projects. The personal -> private_poc transition path is therefore not actually achievable through the documented flag set.

framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh aborts with self-copy cp error during BL-009/BL-015 helper refresh
framework_bugs.reproduction: From a project that has scripts/pending-approval.sh and scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh already present (every project does, after PR #17): cd $PROJECT_DIR && bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --deployment organizational  (or via bash scripts/intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment organizational which execs into the same script). At the very end, the 'Refreshing framework helper scripts (BL-009, BL-015)' block at upgrade-project.sh:1469-1480 does: SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)" ; for helper in pending-approval.sh lint-uat-scenarios.sh; do cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/$helper" "scripts/$helper"; ...; done. When invoked as 'bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh', SCRIPT_DIR resolves to the project's own scripts/ directory, so cp source == cp destination. macOS cp prints 'cp: scripts/pending-approval.sh and .../scripts/pending-approval.sh are identical (not copied).' and exits 1; under 'set -euo pipefail' the entire upgrade script aborts before the final 'Upgrade complete.' message and before the post-upgrade scripts/validate.sh run. The data transformations earlier in the script (phase-state.json, CLAUDE.md, PROJECT_INTAKE.md, APPROVAL_LOG.md, commit) all complete successfully, so the project is left in a partially-upgraded state with the wrapper indicating failure (rc=1). Fix should detect when SCRIPT_DIR == project scripts dir (or when source == destination via realpath) and skip the copy. Alternatively use 'cp -f' is not enough; use a same-file guard like: src=$(realpath "$SCRIPT_DIR/$helper"); dst=$(realpath "scripts/$helper" 2>/dev/null||echo ""); [ "$src" != "$dst" ] && cp ...
framework_bugs.expected: Upgrade completes with exit code 0 and prints 'Upgrade complete.' and runs scripts/validate.sh.
framework_bugs.actual: Upgrade exits 1 just after the 'Refreshing framework helper scripts (BL-009, BL-015)' step header, leaving the user uncertain whether the upgrade succeeded. The data side completed, but the wrapper signal says failure.

framework_bugs.summary: U-J confirmation: deployment field never written to phase-state.json on upgrade
framework_bugs.reproduction: Run any deployment-changing upgrade (e.g. bash scripts/intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment organizational on a personal project), then jq -r '.deployment' .claude/phase-state.json. The python block at scripts/upgrade-project.sh:535-559 writes data['track'] and data['last_upgrade'] but does not write data['deployment'], and TARGET_DEPLOYMENT is not even one of its argv. The CLAUDE.md and PROJECT_INTAKE.md docs DO get the new deployment, but the canonical machine-readable phase-state.json keeps the old value, which any downstream gate or wizard that reads phase-state.json will see as personal forever.
framework_bugs.expected: .claude/phase-state.json '.deployment' equals the new TARGET_DEPLOYMENT after a deployment-changing upgrade.
framework_bugs.actual: .deployment remains 'personal' even after upgrade to organizational completes.

framework_bugs.summary: personal->private_poc upgrade path not reachable via documented flag set
framework_bugs.reproduction: intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment only accepts personal|organizational and forwards to upgrade-project.sh --deployment organizational; that script sets data['deployment']='organizational' but does NOT set poc_mode='private_poc' for the personal->private_poc transition (it only sets poc_mode=sponsored_poc on the explicit --to-sponsored-poc path, or removes poc_mode on --to-production). After the upgrade, jq -r '.poc_mode' .claude/phase-state.json returns null, and there is no documented flag to pass the gov_mode at upgrade time. So the matrix entry 'upgrade personal -> private_poc' has no working CLI path. Either the wizard needs a --gov-mode flag for the upgrade direction, or upgrade-project.sh --deployment organizational needs to default poc_mode to private_poc for personal->organizational transitions (since organizational without a poc_mode is not a valid scenario per the four documented governance modes).
framework_bugs.expected: After upgrading a personal project to private_poc, .claude/phase-state.json has .deployment='organizational' AND .poc_mode='private_poc'.
framework_bugs.actual: .deployment='personal' (U-J) AND .poc_mode=null. Neither field reflects the target.

documentation_gaps.gap: intake-wizard.sh --help advertises only --upgrade-deployment T (personal|organizational); the runbook tells UAT agents to use this command to upgrade to private_poc, but there is no way to pass the target gov_mode through this flag. Either help text should add a --gov-mode private_poc|sponsored_poc|production flag or the runbook command for this transition needs revising.

upgrade-project.sh --help does not document that running the script via scripts/upgrade-project.sh path will trigger the self-copy bug; the script silently aborts at the helper-refresh step. A hint about always invoking via the framework copy is missing.

U-D (no phase-advance script after gate passes) is still open; this UAT agent had to manually edit .current_phase in phase-state.json to reach the Phase 2 baseline. The user-guide does not describe how a new project owner is supposed to advance the phase.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: git grep against committed files for password|api[_-]?key|secret|token shows only documentation/template/tooling references (gitleaks, secret-detection workflow, evaluation-prompts), no actual secret values.

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: N/A — no FORCE/reset events occurred; .claude/process-audit.log absent as expected.

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK status --short shows only the pre-existing untracked .claude/ and Reports/uat-2026-04-26/ entries; HEAD still d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494, matching the pre-test capture. Framework-self guard from PR #18 holds.

summary: personal/web/standard baseline init.sh --non-interactive succeeded cleanly; phase-1 + phase-2 baseline (one feature recorded) built; Section F upgrade to private_poc target surfaced one NEW Critical bug (upgrade-project.sh self-copy in BL-009/BL-015 helper-refresh aborts with rc=1) and re-confirmed U-J (deployment field not written to phase-state.json on upgrade); a documentation-level gap means personal->private_poc has no working CLI flag path (poc_mode stays null). BL-015 sentinel block, framework-self guard, and changelog header all verified working.
